arc: New job shop -> large-scale American manufacturer. You advance by doing tasks CORRECTLY, not just often. Every task is graded; mastery gates every tier.

setup.A: preset tools + correct offsets first try

gcode.F: 3 fails (triggers Shop Class micro-lesson, then retry)

handoff.A: what ran / what's next / what's weird all present

material.A: right alloy, certed when needed, ordered ahead of need

effects.craftsmanship: streaks of B+ build a multiplier (max 2.0x coins); any F resets it
effects.reputation: rolling average of last 20 grades; gates which customers offer you work
effects.learning: every D/F triggers the matching Shop Class micro-lesson before retry - failure always routes to teaching, never a dead end

tiers.name: American Titan
tiers.endState: large-scale manufacturer: multi-building campus, strategic programs, export contracts, and an apprentice academy; the player becomes a mentor without replacing Zach's founding role

antiGrind: Volume without quality stalls you: reputation (grade average) is a hard gate at every tier. The only path up is doing it right.
